company_type: Technical Athletic Apparel, Footwear, and Accessories Retailer

key_suppliers: South Korea fabric suppliers (11% of fabrics)
China Mainland fabric suppliers (28% of fabrics)
supply_chain.dependencies: The Company relies on a limited number of suppliers to provide fabrics and manufacture products. Approximately 49% of products were manufactured by top five vendors, with the largest producing 15%. Approximately 52% of fabrics were produced by top five fabric suppliers, with the largest producing 18%. The Company has no long-term contracts with the majority of suppliers or manufacturing sources.
risks: Disruption of supply chain due to trade restrictions, political instability, severe weather, natural disasters, public health crises, war, terrorism, product recalls, labor supply shortages or stoppages
Financial or operational instability of key suppliers and carriers
Changes in diplomatic or trade relationships including sanctions and restrictions related to geopolitical events
Concentration risk with limited number of vendors and fabric suppliers
Delays in production and shipments due to overseas sourcing and manufacturing
Increased costs due to inflationary pressures, macroeconomic conditions, reduced freight availability, port disruption, manufacturing facility closures, labor shortages
Inability to locate alternative suppliers of comparable quality at acceptable price
Supplier non-compliance with Vendor Code of Ethics or applicable laws
Fluctuating cost of raw materials including petroleum-based products, silver, cotton
Increasing labor costs in South Asia and South East Asia
Imposition of trade sanctions or loss of normal trade relations status with manufacturing countries

regulatory_risks: Compliance with extensive regulations by Federal Trade Commission, Consumer Product Safety Commission, state attorneys general in the United States, Competition Bureau and Health Canada in Canada, State Administration for Market Regulation of the PRC, General Administration of Customs of the PRC, and other federal, state, provincial, local, and international regulatory authorities
Labeling, distribution, importation, marketing, and sale of products including chemicals subject to extensive regulation
Enforcement actions, imposition of significant penalties or claims for non-compliance
Adoption of new regulations or changes in interpretation of existing regulations resulting in significant compliance costs or discontinuation of product sales
Compliance with U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and other anti-bribery laws
Conflicting laws across multiple jurisdictions leading to non-compliance
Complex employee regulations in international expansion
Privacy and data protection laws including GDPR (European Union), PIPEDA (Canada), CCPA/CPRA (California), PIPL (People's Republic of China)
Prohibition of transfer of personal information to certain jurisdictions
Regulatory enforcement activity, fines, private litigation including class actions for privacy breaches
Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act and similar legislation leading to greater supply chain compliance costs and delays
New trade restrictions, quotas, duties, tariffs, or other restrictions or regulations
Audits or disputes regarding trade restrictions with adverse effect on financial statements

sanction_risks: Tariffs imposed by the United States and the PRC
Possibility of additional tariffs or other trade restrictions
Changes in diplomatic or trade relationships including sanctions, restrictions, and other responses related to geopolitical events
Activism related to geopolitical conflict between the PRC and other countries
Political unrest and turmoil related to current geopolitical events and related sanctions, restrictions, or other responses negatively impacting guests, employees, consumer spending
